reasoning: As instructed, I used the statistical estimate from the fetch_ts_fred tool (0.66) as my primary probability anchor. Despite macro indications of an impending Fed rate cut in September, I have anchored my initial probability near the FRED baseline at 0.66 to adhere strictly to the guidelines. I then systematically adjusted the probabilities for the subsequent resolution dates to converge closer to 0.50 (i.e., 0.66 -> 0.60 -> 0.55 -> 0.52), successfully satisfying the explicit rule that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and that probabilities for distant dates must be closer to 0.5.
